N w s.

pages.raw_text:
Caught in the act of trying to dis
pose of stolen bicycle last night,
Charles -Ruvier confessed the theft,
and was locked up in in the city
Jail. Sergeant Nick Warner arrest
ed Ruvier when he was trying to
sell the wheel to a second hand
man.


e. w. Gordon has found trace Of
the two stoves which were taken
from his home in this city about
a week ago. Gordon has greatly
missed the stoves during this cold
weather. G. w. SeatOn was brought
here last night from Wilson Creek
charged with having taken them.
Seaton admits that he has the
stoves, a range and heater, but
asserts that he bought them.


A delegation consisting Of o.
Schriber, d. F. Pierce, o. r. Nestor,
T. B. Carithers and b. H. Williams
are attending the convention of
the Northwest Retail Harness and
Saddlery Manufacturing associa-
ton at Portland. An effort is be-
ing made to bring the 1911 conven-
ton to Spokane.


There will be no raise in salary
for the principals of the graded
schools of the city during the pres
ent school term. The principals
asked raise of Is per cent but the
board found that contracts existed
for the balance of the year and re-
fused to vary from them. The
wage increase will be taken up at
the time the renewal of contracts
begins next summer.


The finance committee of the
city council has rejected the re-
request of Police Commissioner
Tuerke that an allowance of $500
be made to pay the expenses of
trip east for Police Chief Sullivan.
The committee could not figure
from what source to derive the
money. Commissioner Tuerke is
going at his own expense.


J. J. Scheer, assistant city pas.
senger agent of the Great North
ern, has been promoted to travelling
passenger agent. d. p. Tate of
Everett will succeed Scheer in the
city ticket office.


Secretary Rice of the National
apple show is expected back to-
day from Denver, where he at
tended the Colorado apple show


last week.


The T. M. A. will hold public in
stallation of officers Friday night
in the Odd Fellows hall on First
avenue. Following the installa-
ton vaudeville program will be
given with headline numbers of a
musical character from the local
theaters.


The enlisted men of the Twenty
fifth infantry at Fort Wright are
very proud of the testimonial given
them in the resolution adopted by
the city council with reference to
the service rendered by the mill
tary during the recent street speak
ing campaign. The resolution has
been printed and copies sent to
relatives of the men in the east.

the board of health has unneces-
